name: Spiro State Bank
description_short: Spiro State Bank is a locally-owned community bank serving LeFlore County and Eastern Oklahoma since 1913, offering personal and business banking with three convenient locations.
description_long:
Spiro State Bank has operated as a hometown financial institution for over 100 years, establishing itself as a steadfast and progressive bank rooted in LeFlore County and Eastern Oklahoma. The bank was founded in 1913 and continues to operate under local ownership and management, emphasizing personal relationships and community pride as core differentiators from larger regional and national competitors.

The bank offers a comprehensive suite of banking services including personal checking and savings accounts, certificates of deposit, personal loans, and business banking solutions with dedicated deposit accounts. They provide modern digital banking conveniences such as online banking with check ordering, 24/7 mobile banking with alerts and fund transfers, e-statements, and 24-hour telephone banking. Their physical footprint consists of three locations in Spiro, Talihina, and Tulsa, Oklahoma, all equipped with 24-hour ATMs. The bank has recently upgraded to a new online banking system and offers chip-enabled debit cards with enhanced fraud protection.

Spiro State Bank distinguishes itself through its emphasis on being a local, relationship-driven institution where employees maintain strong professional and personal connections with customers. The bank's leadership, including Chairman and CEO Mark Richey and multiple market presidents, are committed to exceeding customer expectations. Their 100-year history provides stability and local knowledge that larger institutions cannot replicate, particularly for customers in LeFlore County and surrounding areas who value community banking.

The bank's primary limitation is its geographic footprint—with only three locations across a limited region—making it inaccessible to customers outside Eastern Oklahoma and the Tulsa metropolitan area. While they offer modern digital banking features, their service menu is more limited compared to large regional banks, and specific details about loan terms, deposit rates, and fee structures are not publicly available on their website, requiring customers to contact branches directly. Many banks offer installment loans with fixed monthly payments, giving borrowers a clear payoff timeline.
services: Mobile banking with fund transfers and alerts
Online check ordering through Harland Clarke
Chip-enabled debit cards with fraud protection
pros: 100+ year history providing stability and deep community roots since 1913
Three convenient physical locations with 24-hour ATM access across the region
Modern online banking platform with mobile app, e-statements, and check ordering
24/7 banking access via telephone and mobile channels for account management
Local ownership and decision-making with dedicated market presidents for each location
Chip-enabled debit cards with enhanced fraud protection and 24/7 fraud monitoring
Full-service banking including personal loans, business loans, and deposit products
cons: Limited geographic footprint restricted to three locations in Eastern Oklahoma and Tulsa only
Website lacks specific information on interest rates, fees, and loan terms requiring direct contact
Smaller institution may have fewer services, products, and technology features compared to regional/national banks
No information provided about online account opening, account minimums, or promotional offers
Limited business loan details—no mention of SBA loans or specialized commercial products
best_for: Residents of LeFlore County, Spiro, Talihina, and Eastern Oklahoma seeking relationship-based community banking
Small business owners in the tri-county area looking for personalized loan and deposit services
Customers who prioritize local decision-making and value long-term banking relationships over national brand recognition
Individuals seeking a stable, locally-owned alternative to large regional and national banks
diagnosis: Spiro State Bank is best for customers in LeFlore County and Eastern Oklahoma who value local ownership, community relationships, and personalized service over the convenience of a large national network. The main caveat is geographic limitation—if you don't live or work near their three Oklahoma locations, this bank is not accessible, and their limited public information on rates and terms requires direct contact with branches to evaluate specific products.
typical_results_timeline: Account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions vary by product.
